steps.title: Fastest check: compare a table or graph
steps.content: For an equivalent-expression question, enter the original expression and the answer choices in a calculator table or graph. The correct choice must match for several inputs, not just one. The matching choice is A, $5x \left(x-10{y}^{2}\right)$.

steps.title: Algebra check
steps.content: Use the work below to confirm the identity for every allowed input. This matters because one random matching input is only a quick screen, not a proof that two expressions are always equivalent.

steps.title: Factor the expression
steps.content: The GCF of $5x^2$ and $50xy^2$ is $5x$. Factor it out:$$5x^2-50xy^2=5x(x-10y^2)$$

steps.title: Match to the answer choices
steps.content: The factored form is $5x(x-10y^2)$. A is correct. Distributing $5x$ back through the parentheses checks both original terms: $5x^2$ and $-50xy^2$.

choiceAnalysis:
• Choice A — $5x \left(x-10{y}^{2}\right)$: Distributing gives $5x \cdot x - 5x \cdot 10y^2 = 5x^2 - 50xy^2$, which matches exactly. ✓
  • Choice B — $5x \left(x-50{y}^{2}\right)$: Distributing gives $5x^2 - 250xy^2$, not $5x^2 - 50xy^2$. ✗
  • Choice C — $5{x}^{2} \left(10x{y}^{2}\right)$: This is a product, not a difference; it equals $50x^3y^2$, not the original expression. ✗
  • Choice D — $5{x}^{2} \left(50x{y}^{2}\right)$: This equals $250x^3y^2$, not the original expression. ✗
A is correct.
